title_original: Silicon Quantum Computing | SQC Launches "Quantum Twins" Enabling Simulation of Quantum Physics and Chemistry
summary_original: Silicon Quantum Computing, based in Sydney, has launched Quantum Twins, an application-specific quantum simulator capable of replicating quantum systems for materials and chemistry research. The product leverages arrays of 15,000 qubit registers, patterned on pure silicon with 0.13 nanometer atomic precision, allowing direct modeling of physical systems beyond classical computer capabilities. Results underpinning the platform were published in Nature. SQC demonstrated the ability to pattern 250,000 qubit registers in eight hours in November 2025, highlighting rapid manufacturing scalability. Founder and CEO Michelle Simmons called the achievement “an incredible achievement in semiconductor manufacturing with sub-nanometer accuracy.” Quantum Twins are now available by direct contract, providing a new tool for quantum analysis and materials discovery.
